item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the whimsical tone of the article, and the subsequent implications for the version of The Foundation featured. There's some absolutely great lines here such as…

My favourite lines
My favourite lines
  • YOU'RE GOING TO LOOK ME IN THE DOME
  • DR. CROCS IS HOSTING A MUTINY.
  • THE MOON IS WHITE, DUMBASS.
  • LOOK HERE BUCKAROO
  • a pair of glasses have been taped to its dome
  • IS, AS THE KIDS SAY, WACK

The ending wasn't particularly strong for me (the role in the office doesn't seem too different from the role in the cafeteria?), I'd echo Croquembouche's comment about wanting to see 5595 do well, as by the end of the article I'm quite fond of the anomaly, and expect other readers are too. I think there was an opportunity to do this with their budget suggestions.

This doesn't significantly effect my enjoyment of the article though, as the main draw is the characterisation of 5595, which is done splendidly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your critique! I've addressed your points here, specifically towards the removal of the HPC, a bit more characterization in the first and second log, spicing up the human dialogue (mainly Lague's), the removal of those final two lines from the first interview, and I changed the second addendum.

Thanks again! This was a real help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have made some changes, hopefully, they've fixed the issues. Thanks for your insight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Votes don't help the author, so I've vowed to always leave a comment with mine, at the very least whenever I rate an article negatively. If you feel the same, help out by leaving critique with more of your votes. Comment follows:

I actually really enjoyed the start of this, but I felt that it started to taper out towards the end of the first interview.

+ show block
– hide block

Lines like "UP AND AT THEM. THAT IS THE SPIRIT." are probably really cliche - I don't have a hard time imagining that this character already exists in the Fallout universe - but the visuals of a gumball machine and imagining those lines in a tinny, electric monotone voice was actually quite funny. You got a laugh out of me at "PLEASE BRING ME TO MY OFFICE. DR. CROIX IS HOSTING A MUTINY." Dialogue from the machine is excellent but dialogue from human characters is lacking. The last two lines in that log from Croix, especially, take me right out of "the foundation is treating this seriously" territory, and take with that my suspension of disbelief for the rest of the article.

The HPC is never really developed or returned to beyond what feels like a mention because there must be a mention, and I ended up wishing that it hadn't been included at all. It felt like an excuse for the interview logs to exist rather than something that develops the plot.

The first interview is used to establish the character and its attitude, which is fine, but everything after that seems aimless. The second interview log feels like filler and the resolution, including current containment procedures, doesn't hinge at all on this article's USP (which is the gumball machine's character). You lampshade the fact that a gumball machine that thinks it's a site director doesn't want to be doing detective work, but it's still what happens, and it's not really justified. Nor is Lague's line of thinking - he's just fabricated an excuse to get the gumball machine out of containment, and it's one that potentially puts his staff at risk. Even saying that, the status quo barely changes, and we don't get to explore what any of the characters actually want. We just reach a happy, meaningless resolution, with no conflict or interesting outcomes as a result.

What I wanted was a funny gumball machine that asserts its assumed position to the point of changing something (I'm imagining a log of some sorts featuring Lague grovelling for his job back from a machine). What I got was a funny gumball machine. It's a promising premise but there's too much stuff happening here that's just not funny, and not enough that is. It's a -1 overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does the black moon howl?
THE MOON IS WHITE, DUMBASS.

Favorite part of the scip. Loved it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. As far as the Foundation knows, the HPC is (and they are) benevolent, albeit slightly pushy. Further, 5595 hasn't been given any useful information nor knows anything (should it be transmitting info to the HPC) and is just an overzealous and overconfident gumball machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right, but that still seems bizarrely naive and antithetical to the whole enterprise given that this thing was deliberately inveigled into a Foundation site by some entity that apparently has knowledge of its organisational structure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thoroughly enjoyed this one, made me smile. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-5595 is to be contained within the Site-322 cafeteria and is allowed to roam freely within it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems really dumb for The Foundation to give a planted anomaly a hall pass to freely wander around a site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read a story about MTF 950 ( ALL OUT OF BUBBLEGUM)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i really enjoyed this, made me laugh more than once. i like to think that he'll actually discover something of note in the cafeteria some day. easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: PERHAPS

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But does the white moon howl?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed! Go ahead and write about the HPC, I’d love to see it! If you need any pointers feel free to contact me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Ah, much has been said about this SCP and how lovely it is, and I must agree, of course. But I'm quite intquiged by the GOI introduced here, and Happy Pals Command has a good ring to it. If you don't mind, I might be interested in writing a tale about the HPC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not too long, got me laughing and a genuine good read. What more do you need. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be ObsidianBook. It's staff policy to blank posts of users that have been banned for plagarism, as you can see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait, is ObsidianBook the one one getting banned or is it PladuePJP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for plagiarism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks. appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good. upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the read! I’ll get to fixing these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fair, though it only says it 5 times in total throughout the entire piece. Boiling this piece down to 4 lines of dialogue within 30 60 is odd. I can see how it may get old and that’s why I didn’t push it that far.

Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's very cute; I love those interview logs. However, there's something about the description section that just feels… Off? Here's what stood out to me:

SCP-5595 is an unbranded gumball machine fitted with a steel body and glass dome that presumably acts as its visual and auditory organs.

Why is it presumed? It sounds like the Foundation is just making assumptions. Maybe say that statements made by our lovely Director Harrison suggest they function as such.

SCP-5595 is sapient and is capable of intelligent speach via a hidden speaker system in its main body. Although, its voice highly modulated and barely intelligible at times.

That would work better reworked into a single sentence, I think.

Recovery protocols were enacted, SCP-5595 was captured with relative ease and placed in a Safe-class containment chamber.

Comma splice, and that "relative ease" feels a bit off tone.

Not enough to keep me from a +1; this is adorably charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thought I'd revisit this piece since I agree that boiling the dialogue to just a few lines is odd. Unfortunately, those few lines are the most memorable parts of the article — in a bad way. And of the piece's remaining
lines of dialogue, only two are actually funny.

Notable points:

+ show block
– hide block

5595 only gets 27 lines. Eight of these are exceedingly short: basic answers like "YES", "What do you mean", "I would not like that", and "Not yet". So less than twenty of its lines actually characterize it, which means a full fifth of 5595's dialogue reiterates that it is "SITE DIRECTOR GEOFFREY HARRISON THE THIRD", which tells me that you intended to lean on this for humor but failed. Instead of being charmingly dumb, it's an unpleasantly grating introduction.

It's worse because this that dialogue is front-loaded right at the beginning — three of the first four lines repeat that point. That's a full third of 5595's dialogue in the first interview log alone. That wouldn't be a problem if the rest of the dialogue was funny or even memorable, but it isn't. The only funny lines it has are "THE MOON IS WHITE, DUMBASS", and "LOOK HERE BUCKAROO, I'M A GOOD ASSET TO HAVE."

The interviewers' dialogue is similarly unmemorable. The first interviewer primarily reiterates that 5595 is not a site director, which isn't made funnier by its repetition. The second interviewer's dialogue starts to pick up near the end when he threatens the machine, but most of it consists of pointless filler questions and statements rather than funny repartee.

The addendum could still potentially salvage the piece… but it doesn't. The potato-cauliflower mishap, "buy me a drink first", and the punchline about a diet program all feel like lazy gags rather than additional fun characterization.

5595's dialogue is clearly the focal point of this piece but there's not enough that's actually enjoyable. A third of its lines are basic five-word sentences. So of the sixty lines of dialogue, less than twenty actually characterize it. And when a fifth of those twenty are just 5595 repeating its long, boring name over and over again… it's hard not to be disappointed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed thanks!

Glad I made you laugh!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5595: [[size 96%]] I WILL AGREE TO THAT.

formatting error. besides that,

THE MOON IS WHITE, DUMBASS.

I laughed. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been watching Regular Show for a bit and I found it rather interesting that a gumball machine was managing a park. I also have had the Happy Pals Command in my head for a bit so here's the mash-up of these two odd sectors of my mind.

This was initially posted about two weeks ago, I self-deleted due to criticism about the ending, characterization, and all that jazz. I did some reworks, got a better ending, and a bit more humor. May I tell you, I'm much happier with this piece this time around.

Special Thanks
cybersqyd, Cerastes, Flopmind, KaraKatt, OCuin, Nagiros, and Dr Moned

I'd like to especially thank chiifu who I made read this thing maybe 5 or 6 times.

Plague's Laboratory

ETA: Shoutout to REDESERT for the spotlight!
